extraction.one_line_summary: PIDCOCK attacked solo from the breakaway on the final HC climb to Alpe d'Huez, held off MEINTJES and FROOME for his first Tour stage win.
tactical_patterns: POWLESS attacked immediately in neutral section before official start, revealing aggressive early-break positioning strategy.
VINGEGAARD fought back multiple times to limit losses on Alpe d'Huez, prioritizing podium over stage hunting.
FROOME finished top-3 for first time since February 2019, signaling return to competitive form after 2019 crash.

rider_signals.PIDCOCK Thomas: positive: First Grand Tour stage win at 23, elevated from outside top-10 to 8th GC, confirming rapid trajectory
rider_signals.POWLESS Neilson: positive: Attacked in neutral section and contested intermediate sprints despite breakaway odds, shows exceptional aggression
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: First top-3 finish since pre-2019 crash, completed stage despite earlier difficulties, rehabilitation nearly complete
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: Clawed back to group multiple times on final climb to defend yellow by 2:22 over POGAČAR
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: Moved from 3rd to 2nd GC, closed gap on VINGEGAARD in high mountains
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: Moved 4th to 3rd GC, passed ROGLIČ in mountain stage, positioning for podium
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: negative: Dropped from 2nd to 4th GC, lost ground to THOMAS and POGAČAR on Alpine stage
rider_signals.YATES Adam: negative: Lost ground on Alpe d'Huez but commentary suggests Alps weakness may reverse in Pyrenees
